creator: shin@kame.net
text:
At the extent of current information,
          mozilla6 can access apache6 by IPv6 on the same LAN.
          It can't from the different LAN.
        From the information by telnet on port 80,
          paradise.v6.kame.net can get information from
          kiwi6.v6.itojun.org exceprt for
          "Host: kiwi6.v6.itojun.org" command.
          When the command exists in the message from paradise,
          kiwi6 doesn't answer.
          On the other hand,
          kiwi6.v6.itojun.org can get no information from
          paradise.v6.kame.net by telnet on port 80.
          Paradise gives no answer for every command.
          (only, telnet on port 80 connection can be established)
        By the way, mozilla6 can access remote www.v6.hitachi.co.jp
        by IPv6 smoothly.
        And kiwi6 and paradise can access him by telnet on port 80,
        and also every command is OK, including "Host: hoge" command.

Fix: 

unknown.
How-To-Repeat:         Access kiwi6.v6.itojun.org by mozilla6 on v6.kame.net.
        Reverse is not checked. Another kind of example is very
        welcome.
